title: palaquium thwaitesii
intro: Palaquium thwaitesii, also known as the Sri Lankan or Thwaites' palaquium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a small tree or shrub native to Sri Lanka, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Palaquium thwaitesii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal benefits, and its herbal preparations have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
